blockers.description: Tests fail: processEvolutionQueue now delegates to unmocked EvolutionTaskDispatcher. Fake timers work but the dispatcher private methods don't trigger expected state changes in queue.
blockers.workaround: Queue object reference may not be shared correctly - load() returns queue but private methods modify in-place

decisions.decision: Created thin processEvolutionQueue wrapper for backward compat

decisions.rationale: Recovery modifications were not persisted before

next_action: Debug _recoverStuckSleepReflection: queue object reference from store.load() passed to private method - in-place modification should work but test shows status='in_progress' not 'failed'
context_notes: The _recoverStuckSleepReflection modifies queue in-place and store.save() was called. But test still shows 'in_progress'. Possible issue: vi.useFakeTimers() may not control Date.now() inside the dispatcher's async call chain. The processEvolutionQueue wrapper is synchronous but dispatcher.dispatchQueue is async - test waits 5000ms with advanceTimersByTimeAsync but the async operations may not complete within fake time.
